title: Update native LICHEN BLE client discovery for LCI UUIDs
description:
Update native client/tooling discovery notes and implementation so clients probe the LICHEN-specific native BLE LCI service before legacy NUS, never use NUS fallback when MeshCore compatibility is advertised, and surface protocol version/capability reads from the direct BLE LCI service.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Native clients or discovery documentation probe the LICHEN-specific native BLE LCI service before legacy NUS.
- Clients do not use native NUS fallback when MeshCore compatibility is advertised.
- Client-visible behavior includes protocol version and four-octet capabilities reads.
- Migration behavior for legacy mutually exclusive native NUS images is documented or tested.
notes:
Implementation evidence 2026-07-02:
- Updated Python native BLE client transport/discovery for direct LICHEN BLE LCI UUIDs. Discovery now matches profiles in caller-provided priority order, so callers can enable migration fallback as (LICHEN_LCI_PROFILE, NUS_LCI_PROFILE) and still prefer LICHEN-specific LCI when a device advertises both.
- Added MeshCore NUS suppression: NUS_LCI_PROFILE candidates are skipped when device name, advertisement local_name, or metadata local_name advertises MeshCore compatibility. Metadata local_name sequence values are flattened so metadata-only scanners do not accidentally accept MeshCore NUS as native LCI.
- Added client-visible direct BLE LCI metadata: BlePacketTransport.metadata, BleLciMetadata, read_lci_metadata(), LICHEN_LCI_VERSION, and LICHEN_LCI_CAPABILITY_SLIP_IPV6. Direct LCI connect reads 2-byte little-endian protocol version and exactly 4-byte little-endian capabilities before subscribing to TX. Legacy NUS profile has no metadata UUIDs and skips reads.
- Exported BleLciMetadata, read_lci_metadata, LICHEN_LCI_VERSION, and LICHEN_LCI_CAPABILITY_SLIP_IPV6 from lichen.client.

Tests/checks:
- cd python && uv run pytest tests/client/test_ble.py -q -> 23 passed in 0.04s.
- cd python && uv run pytest tests/client -q -> 47 passed in 0.07s.
- cd python && uv run ruff check src/lichen/client/ble.py src/lichen/client/__init__.py tests/client/test_ble.py -> pass.
- cd python && uv run mypy src/lichen/client/ble.py tests/client/test_ble.py -> success, no issues found.
- git diff --check -> pass.
- No EC2 needed; Python-only no-hardware client/tooling change.

Test coverage added:
- Native LCI metadata version/capability reads on connect.
- Malformed version length and malformed 3-byte capabilities rejection.
- Legacy NUS metadata reads skipped for read_lci_metadata() and BlePacketTransport(profile=NUS_LCI_PROFILE).
- Default discovery returns only LICHEN-specific LCI.
- Dual-advertised native+NUS device selects LICHEN-specific LCI even when NUS appears first.
- Explicit legacy NUS profile still discovers NUS-only legacy devices.
- MeshCore NUS suppression via device name, advertisement local_name, and metadata-only local_name list.

Review evidence:
- Explorer Darwin identified existing behavior and gaps: no metadata reads, no MeshCore guard, set-order discovery priority, and missing legacy migration tests.
- Test-design Nash proposed focused tests for native-vs-NUS priority, MeshCore suppression, metadata reads, malformed capabilities, legacy metadata skip, and explicit NUS migration.
- Harvey review: no P0/P1/P2; ran python3 -m pytest python/tests/client/test_ble.py -> 22 passed before final regressions.
- Confucius review found P2: MeshCore suppression missed metadata[local_name] sequence values on metadata-only scanner path; fixed by flattening values and adding regression. Re-review clean; pytest tests/client/test_ble.py -> 23 passed.
- Rawls review: no P0/P1/P2 after MeshCore metadata fix; pytest tests/client/test_ble.py -> 23 passed.
- Bernoulli final review: no P0/P1/P2; verified pytest, ruff, and mypy.
- Poincare export-focused review: no P0/P1/P2; public exports consistent; python3 -m pytest python/tests/client/test_ble.py -> 23 passed.
close_reason: Updated Python native BLE client discovery and transport metadata behavior: LICHEN-specific LCI is preferred over legacy NUS, MeshCore NUS fallback is suppressed, direct LCI version/capabilities are read and exposed, and legacy NUS remains explicit opt-in. Python checks and independent reviews passed.
